pairs.question: What was the original purpose of the Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotives?
pairs.answer: The Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotives were designed for hauling goods trains.

pairs.question: How many Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotives were included in the Deutsche Reichsbahn's Class 89.7?
pairs.answer: 17 Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotives were included in the Deutsche Reichsbahn's Class 89.7.

pairs.question: What were the designations of the second series of Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotives under the Deutsche Reichsbahn?
pairs.answer: The second series of Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotives were designated as Class 89.8 under the Deutsche Reichsbahn.

pairs.question: How many units of the second series Bavarian Class R 3/3 were taken over by the Deutsche Bundesbahn after World War II?
pairs.answer: 86 units of the second series Bavarian Class R 3/3 were taken over by the Deutsche Bundesbahn after World War II.

pairs.question: Which specific Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotive was later found in the Bavarian Railway Museum in Nördlingen?
pairs.answer: Number 789.837 was found in the Bavarian Railway Museum in Nördlingen.

pairs.question: What happened to the Bavarian Class R 3/3 locomotive No. 89 801 during the fire at Nuremberg in 2005?
pairs.answer: No. 89 801 was damaged during the fire at Nuremberg on 17 October 2005.

pairs.question: How can we determine the current status of locomotive No. 89 801?
pairs.answer: Locomotive No. 89 801 is currently being restored in Koblenz as part of the DB Museum fleet.
